svg.title: Gemba walk — active decision on mk-e6 Pocket API v1 deprecation. Two-pane: agenda + chat.

g.text: · 2 of 5 decided · started 6m ago by Mike · $0.04 this walk
Ratified: stage in parallel group A
added by Mike at walk start
HIGH · raised by Code Reviewer Coach 8m ago
Pocket retired their v1 API last week. mk-e6 as scoped assumes v1. Three options I see:
Update mk-e6 to target Pocket v2 — shape is similar; adds ~8k tokens to the epic.
Defer mk-e6; focus on Raindrop-only import first — smaller scope, ships sooner.
Cancel mk-e6 and file a fresh raindrop_import epic.
My read: (a) keeps scope discipline and matches your Execution value. I lean (a).
Consulted Architect, Deployment Engineer, Documentarian (perspectives inline below).
"Option (a) keeps scope discipline; Pocket v2 has similar shape. Migration is mostly rename + pagination."
"Option (b) ships faster but fragments the import story. Users won't see Pocket support v1 → v2 transition."
"Whichever path, decisions-log needs an entry within 24h per freshness guardrail."
2 more perspectives (QA, Security) collapsed · expand all
Lean (a). What's the risk if Pocket v2 changes again?
chat surface pinned above — input lives here during walks
Type to reply during walk… (⌘↵ to send)
